title: lippia globiflora var. geminata
intro: Lippia globiflora var. geminata, also known as Mexican Oregano, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the verbena family and is native to Mexico and parts of Central America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and treat various infections. The health benefits of Mexican Oregano include its ability to alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce stress and promote relaxation. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infused oil, or dried leaves for use in cooking and as a natural remedy.
